TREETOP. GROWTH STRATEGY Confidential · Appendix
Evidence Appendix

R3BAR : Evidence Appendix

June 16, 2026, full per-ad teardowns.

Growth is a system, not a series of bets.
How to read each ad

The seven dimensions

Hook. What stops the scroll in the first seconds.
Angle. The core problem or desire it leads with.
Visual Format. How the creative is built (UGC, demo, lifestyle...).
Copy Framework. The persuasion structure at work.
CTA. The ask, and how hard it pushes.
Emotional Trigger. The primary emotion it activates.
What Makes It Work. The core mechanic driving its effectiveness.
Gorilla Fitness 123 days live Meta Ad Library →
Primary: 'A workout you actually stick with. 30 minutes. At home. Full body.' | Headline: 'Up to 30% Off' | CTA: 'Shop now' | Brand logo: 'GORILLA BOW'MediumVideo-Produced
Hook
Extreme close-up macro shots of the Gorilla Bow being unboxed/assembled, the tactile, textured visuals of bands and equipment demand attention before the product is even revealed.
Angle
Consistency and convenience: leading with the desire for a workout routine you'll actually stick to, positioning the Gorilla Bow as the solution to workout abandonment and gym avoidance.
Visual Format
Lifestyle/product demo hybrid, cinematic close-up macro shots of the product transitioning into an athletic female model using the Gorilla Bow outdoors at a park, ending on a clean brand logo card.
Copy Framework
Desire-first with implied Before/After: opens by naming the core pain point (workouts you don't stick with), then presents the product as the frictionless solution, '30 minutes, at home, full body' acts as a three-punch benefit stack removing all common objections.
CTA
'Shop Now' with 'Up to 30% Off' discount urgency framing, discount is the incentive layer that converts aspiration into action.
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration combined with relief, the viewer wants the fit, active lifestyle shown on screen and feels relief that this product removes the barriers (time, gym, complexity) standing between them and that identity.
What Makes It Work
The teaser-style macro close-ups create a curiosity gap that forces the viewer to watch longer before the product is revealed, while the copy directly names the #1 reason people quit working out (lack of adherence) and instantly resolves it with a simple, time-bound promise.
X3 Bar 46 days live Meta Ad Library →
Headline: 'X3 brought the gym to me. Forever Grateful', Christian Shepard | CTA: Shop Now | Brand bar (orange) top and bottom with no additional visible copy in the creative itselfLowStatic-Image
Hook
A grid of 35 shirtless mirror selfies showing a dramatic physical transformation from soft/average physique to lean, muscular body, the visual volume of proof is the hook itself, stopping the scroll through sheer overwhelming quantity of evidence
Angle
Real, sustained body transformation results achieved at home, leading with the desire for a gym-quality physique without a gym, validated by one man's documented multi-month journey
Visual Format
Static collage/grid of UGC-style mirror selfies arranged chronologically (top-to-bottom, left-to-right) showing progressive body recomposition, framed by bold orange brand-color bars top and bottom
Copy Framework
Before/After social proof at massive scale, instead of a single before/after, the ad deploys a 35-frame timeline that functions as an irrefutable proof cascade, combined with a named testimonial quote ('X3 brought the gym to me. Forever Grateful' - Christian Shepard) to add identity and credibility
CTA
'Shop Now', framed passively after the visual evidence does all the selling; the transformation grid pre-sells so heavily that the CTA simply captures already-converted intent
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration layered with social proof validation, the viewer sees themselves in the 'before' frames and projects themselves into the 'after' frames, while the volume of documented progress creates trust and eliminates skepticism
What Makes It Work
The ad weaponizes quantity as credibility, 35 documented progress photos create an undeniable, tamper-resistant proof stack that a single before/after could never achieve. The slow, visible progression reads as authentic and earned, making the transformation feel attainable rather than extraordinary.
Gorilla Fitness 194 days live Meta Ad Library →
No visible on-screen text in the creative image itself; ad library copy: 'Transform any space into your ultimate home gym, Gorilla Bow's rugged resistance system delivers full‑body strength training wherever you are. Lightweight, portable, and engineered for every fitness level.' | Headline: 'Trucker Hat Gorilla Bow Style'MediumStatic-Image
Hook
A confident, smiling fit man wearing branded Gorilla Bow trucker hat and t-shirt in a gym setting, the lifestyle identity visual IS the hook, signaling 'this is who you become'
Angle
Aspirational identity and brand belonging, leads with the desire to look and feel like a serious, fit, gym-culture insider who reps the Gorilla Bow brand proudly
Visual Format
Lifestyle/brand ambassador static image, single close-up shot of an athletic male model wearing branded merchandise (hat + tee) in a gym environment with soft bokeh background
Copy Framework
Aspiration + Social Proof by association, the primary text frames the product ecosystem (home gym system), while the creative sells identity adoption; no explicit PAS, instead uses lifestyle mirroring to create desire
CTA
'Shop Now', framed as an entry point to the branded merchandise/lifestyle, low-friction impulse purchase positioned as joining the tribe rather than buying a product
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration and belonging, the smiling, fit model activates the viewer's desire to identify with the Gorilla Bow fitness community and project that same confident, athletic identity
What Makes It Work
The ad sells identity before it sells product, by showing a relatable yet aspirational figure genuinely smiling in branded gear, it makes the viewer want to BE that person, not just buy a hat. The dual logo placement (hat + shirt) reinforces brand recognition subliminally while the gym backdrop validates the lifestyle authenticity.
Gorilla Fitness 123 days live Meta Ad Library →
Primary text: 'A workout you actually stick with. 30 minutes. At home. Full body.' | Headline: 'Up to 30% Off' | CTA: 'Shop now' | End card: Gorilla Bow logoMediumVideo-Produced
Hook
Extreme close-up macro shots of the Gorilla Bow being unboxed/unpacked with tactile detail, the dark material and bungee cords fill the screen, creating visceral curiosity about what the product is before it's revealed.
Angle
Desire-led: a portable, full-body resistance workout you'll actually stay consistent with, framed around the aspirational outcome of a fit physique achievable at home in 30 minutes.
Visual Format
Produced lifestyle/product demo hybrid, opens with cinematic macro product detail shots (unboxing reveal tease), transitions to an attractive fit female athlete using the Gorilla Bow outdoors in a park, ends on a clean brand logo card.
Copy Framework
Aspiration + Simplicity Promise: leads with the consistency problem ('a workout you actually stick with'), then resolves it with a simple, achievable formula (30 min / at home / full body), closing with a scarcity/urgency discount offer (up to 30% off).
CTA
'Shop Now' paired with 'Up to 30% Off', framed as a time-sensitive discount incentive to drive immediate purchase action.
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration, the visually fit female athlete using the product in an outdoor lifestyle setting activates the viewer's desire to achieve a similar physique and active lifestyle, while the simplicity promise reduces the intimidation barrier.
What Makes It Work
The ad opens with tactile macro mystery shots that force curiosity before the product is revealed, then immediately anchors desire to a relatable consistency pain point. The combination of an aspirational fit body, outdoor lifestyle authenticity, and a concrete simple promise (30 min / full body / at home) makes the product feel both attainable and transformative.
Gorilla Fitness 33 days live Meta Ad Library →
X BUSY SCHEDULES | amazing shape from home | I have 360 lbs of resistance | all of which I can do from home | you must consider the Gorilla Bow | GORILLA BOW [logo] | SHOP NOWLowVideo-UGC
Hook
'X BUSY SCHEDULES' overlaid on a blurred calendar, immediately signals 'this solves the time problem that's killing your fitness goals'
Angle
Busy schedules and lack of gym access are the enemy, you can get in amazing shape from home without sacrificing your lifestyle
Visual Format
Multi-creator UGC mashup: text-on-screen problem statement, male fitness influencer demonstrating the product in a home gym, second male creator using it in a garage, female creator outdoors, all stitched together with bold subtitle-style captions
Copy Framework
PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solve): Opens by calling out the #1 objection (busy schedule), agitates with the implication that traditional gym solutions don't fit your life, then presents Gorilla Bow as the specific solution, backed by a specific proof point (360 lbs of resistance) and multiple UGC creators validating 'from home' use cases
CTA
'SHOP NOW' button on final brand card with logo and product spread, framed passively after social proof saturation; the ad library headline 'Up to 30% Off Sitewide' adds urgency and discount incentive
Emotional Trigger
Relief and aspiration, relief that busy schedules are no longer a barrier, combined with aspiration triggered by the shirtless, athletic creator physique as the achievable outcome
What Makes It Work
The ad weaponizes the audience's #1 stated excuse (no time/no gym) in the very first frame, instantly qualifying viewers who feel that pain, then uses a specific resistance number (360 lbs) as a credibility anchor that makes the product feel as serious as a real gym. Multiple real-world home/garage settings reinforce the core promise that this is genuinely doable anywhere.
Gorilla Fitness 47 days live Meta Ad Library →
Primary text: 'A workout you actually stick with. 30 minutes. At home. Full body.' | Headline: 'Up to 30% Off' | CTA: 'Shop now' | Logo card: 'GORILLA BOW'MediumVideo-Produced
Hook
Extreme close-up macro shots of resistance bands and fitness equipment being handled/assembled, the unusual tight framing creates immediate curiosity about what the product actually is, forcing the viewer to keep watching to orient themselves.
Angle
Workout consistency and simplicity: the ad leads with the desire for a sustainable fitness routine you'll actually stick to, not extreme transformation, but a practical 30-minute full-body solution you can do at home.
Visual Format
Produced lifestyle video with cinematic close-up macro shots transitioning to full-body athlete footage of a fit woman using the Gorilla Bow resistance system outdoors, ending on a clean logo card. Mix of product detail reveals and aspirational fitness content.
Copy Framework
Desire-led benefit stacking: leads with the core emotional promise ('a workout you actually stick with'), then layers in specificity (30 minutes, at home, full body) to reduce objections, followed by a time-sensitive discount offer to drive urgency. No explicit problem stated, pure aspiration with friction removal.
CTA
'Shop Now' button paired with 'Up to 30% Off' headline, framed as a limited discount opportunity, creating urgency around an already desirable product.
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration combined with relief, the promise that this is a workout you'll actually maintain removes the guilt and frustration of past fitness failures, while the athletic lifestyle imagery fuels aspirational identity appeal.
What Makes It Work
The disorienting macro-first visual strategy hijacks scroll behavior by forcing curiosity before the product is even revealed, buying critical watch-time. The copy then converts that attention with a low-barrier, high-specificity promise (30 min, home, full body) that makes the purchase feel like an obvious, guilt-free solution.
Gorilla Fitness 194 days live Meta Ad Library →
❌ BUSY SCHEDULES | and helped me get into | I have 360 lbs of resistance | AND LEG EXERCISES | you must consider the Gorilla Bow | GORILLA BOW [logo] | SHOP NOWMediumVideo-UGC
Hook
❌ BUSY SCHEDULES, bold white text with red X overlaid on a blurred calendar/planner, instantly calling out the viewer's #1 excuse for not working out
Angle
Eliminating the 'I'm too busy / no time for the gym' objection by positioning the Gorilla Bow as the solution that lets you train anywhere on your schedule, no gym required
Visual Format
Mixed UGC + product demo: opens with text-on-screen over a blurred calendar, transitions to shirtless male creator doing exercises with the Gorilla Bow in a home gym, then a female user outdoors on a rooftop, closes with a branded product showcase end card displaying the full band kit
Copy Framework
PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solve): Problem = busy schedule prevents consistent training; Agitate = implied lost gains/fitness goals; Solve = Gorilla Bow delivers 360 lbs of resistance for full-body + leg exercises anywhere, social proof layered in via two different real-user testimonial-style demonstrations
CTA
'SHOP NOW' button on final branded end card, framed passively after a complete product reveal showing all resistance bands and carry bag, low-pressure but visually conclusive
Emotional Trigger
Relief + aspiration: relief from the guilt of skipping the gym due to a busy schedule, combined with aspiration of achieving a strong, lean physique without traditional gym constraints
What Makes It Work
The red-X calendar hook immediately intercepts the viewer's primary self-identified excuse, creating instant identity-level resonance before a single product feature is shown. The specific '360 lbs of resistance' stat combined with two diverse real-user demos (male gym setting, female outdoor setting) builds credibility and broadens the addressable audience in one continuous narrative.
Gorilla Fitness 126 days live Meta Ad Library →
❌ BUSY SCHEDULES | and helped me get into | I have 360 lbs of resistance | AND LEG EXERCISES | you must consider the Gorilla Bow | SHOP NOWMediumVideo-UGC
Hook
❌ BUSY SCHEDULES, Bold red X crossed over a calendar/planner, immediately calling out the #1 excuse people use for skipping workouts in the first 2 seconds.
Angle
Pain-point elimination: 'You don't have time for the gym' is the enemy, and the Gorilla Bow destroys that excuse by delivering a full home gym workout (360 lbs resistance) that fits any schedule.
Visual Format
UGC-style talking head / product demo hybrid, shirtless male demonstrating exercises with the Gorilla Bow in a home gym, female user shown outdoors on a rooftop, ending with a branded product showcase frame with colorful resistance bands laid out.
Copy Framework
PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solve): Opens by agitating the problem (busy schedules), transitions into social proof via personal testimony ('helped me get into shape'), builds desire with a specific credibility stat (360 lbs of resistance), then closes with product reveal and hard CTA.
CTA
'SHOP NOW', displayed in a large pill-shaped button on the final branded end card alongside the full Gorilla Bow product lineup, framed as the logical next step after seeing the product in action.
Emotional Trigger
Relief and aspiration, the viewer feels the tension of their busy schedule being 'solved,' combined with aspirational desire triggered by a lean, athletic physique demonstrating the product.
What Makes It Work
The opening pattern-interrupt (red X on a calendar) instantly mirrors the viewer's self-justification for not exercising, creating immediate psychological identification. The specific quantified claim (360 lbs resistance) then converts emotional resonance into rational permission to buy.
X3 Bar 17 days live Meta Ad Library →
X3 Bar Users Group | Dan Wilde · 16m | Let's see those office X3 workouts! No excuses with the X3 bar!!LowStatic-Image
Hook
'Let's see those office X3 workouts! No excuses with the X3 bar!!', stops scroll by combining community challenge with relatable real-world setting (an actual office/workplace), immediately signaling 'you can do this anywhere'
Angle
Eliminating the #1 excuse for skipping workouts, no time, no gym access, by proving the product works in a mundane, everyday environment like a workplace storage room
Visual Format
UGC screenshot overlay, a Facebook community group post screenshotted and presented as ad creative, showing two side-by-side photos of a visibly muscular man using resistance bands in what appears to be a cluttered office/workplace setting
Copy Framework
Social proof + aspirational contrast, leverages a real user community post (authenticity signal) paired with a physically impressive physique to create a before/after implication: 'this product builds a body like this, even without a gym'
CTA
No explicit CTA button visible in the creative itself; the community framing ('Let's see those...') acts as an implicit engagement hook, with the ad-library CTA being 'Shop Now' framed around scientific superiority
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration + belonging, viewer aspires to the physique shown, while the community group format triggers a sense of belonging to a tribe of dedicated users who find creative ways to train anywhere
What Makes It Work
The contrast between the elite physique and the unglamorous office setting creates a powerful cognitive dissonance that makes the product feel both credible and accessible, if this guy looks like THAT training in a storage room, the product must be exceptional. The community group screenshot format adds organic authenticity that bypasses ad skepticism entirely.
Gorilla Fitness 189 days live Meta Ad Library →
GORILLA BOW (logo on bag) | Headline: Ultra Heavy Band Kit / 340 lb | Primary: Transform any space into your ultimate home gym, Gorilla Bow's rugged resistance system delivers full-body strength training wherever you are. Lightweight, portable, and engineered for every fitness level.MediumStatic-Image
Hook
Ultra Heavy Band Kit / 340 lb, the extreme resistance number (340 lb) stops serious fitness buyers mid-scroll by signaling elite-level capability immediately
Angle
Aspiration and capability upgrade, leads with the desire to access serious, heavy-resistance training at home without a commercial gym, positioning the product as a professional-grade solution for committed athletes
Visual Format
Clean flat-lay product shot on pure white background, four color-coded resistance bands (two orange, two purple) symmetrically arranged around a branded Gorilla Bow drawstring storage bag, no human presence, no lifestyle context
Copy Framework
Benefit-led product showcase with implied transformation promise, the primary text uses a before/after framing ('transform any space') paired with feature-stacking (rugged, lightweight, portable, all fitness levels), anchored by a bold spec claim (340 lb) that functions as social proof of performance
CTA
'Shop now', framed as an immediate direct-purchase action, positioned after aspirational copy that has already pre-sold the transformation outcome, reducing friction by keeping the ask simple and transactional
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration combined with empowerment, activates the desire to own serious, professional-grade equipment and the confidence that comes with having the tools to train at the highest level from anywhere
What Makes It Work
The 340 lb resistance claim does the heavy lifting as a credibility anchor, instantly communicating elite performance to strength-focused buyers who self-select. The symmetrical, clinical flat-lay presentation mirrors premium supplement and gear brand aesthetics, signaling quality and justifying purchase intent without needing lifestyle imagery.
Gorilla Fitness 126 days live Meta Ad Library →
GORILLA BOW (on bag logo) | Headline: Ultra Heavy Band Kit / 340 lb | Sub-headline: Your compact home gym | Primary text: Train anywhere, anytime. The Gorilla Bow packs a full gym into one portable system 💪 | CTA: Shop nowMediumStatic-Image
Hook
Ultra Heavy Band Kit / 340 lb, the sheer resistance number (340 lb) stops the scroll by signaling serious, gym-level power in a compact product shot
Angle
Desire-led: replaces the expensive, space-consuming gym with a portable, high-resistance system, freedom and convenience without sacrificing intensity
Visual Format
Clean white-background flat-lay product shot showcasing 4 resistance bands (2 orange, 2 purple) symmetrically arranged around a branded Gorilla Bow drawstring bag, pure product catalog aesthetic
Copy Framework
Value proposition + benefit stacking: leads with portability ('train anywhere, anytime'), anchors credibility with the 340 lb resistance claim, and frames the product as a complete solution ('full gym into one portable system'), a compressed FAB (Features-Advantages-Benefits) structure
CTA
'Shop now', framed as an immediate, low-friction purchase prompt paired with the aspirational sub-headline 'Your compact home gym' to prime intent before the click
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration combined with relief, the viewer aspires to serious strength training while feeling relieved that they no longer need a gym membership or dedicated space
What Makes It Work
The '340 lb' spec does heavy lifting as a pattern interrupt, instantly qualifying serious fitness buyers who equate pound-resistance with legitimacy. The symmetrical, organized flat-lay visually communicates completeness and portability, making the 'full gym in one bag' claim self-evident without a single word of explanation.
Gorilla Fitness 123 days live Meta Ad Library →
Frame 1: 'Morning workout in the living room' | Frame 7: 'Home. Garage. Anywhere. Your gym goes where you go.' | Primary text: 'Every January starts the same. And too many plans end the same way. This year, build a strength routine you can actually maintain. Gorilla Bow removes friction so consistency comes easier 🔁 For a limited time, get 20% off + 1 month of membership included' | Headline: 'Stop Restarting Every Year'LowVideo-UGC
Hook
"Morning workout in the living room", opens on an aspirational, well-decorated living room with no gym equipment visible, creating a curiosity gap: how does this ordinary space become a gym?
Angle
Convenience and consistency: the core pain point is that people keep restarting fitness routines because traditional gyms create friction. The ad leads with the desire for a sustainable, no-excuses workout solution that fits into real life, right in your living room.
Visual Format
UGC-style talking head hybrid with product demo segments, filmed vertically in a real home setting, showing product unboxing from a carry bag, live exercise demonstrations (rows, squats), and direct-to-camera explanation with the product displayed on the floor.
Copy Framework
PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solution): Primary text opens with the cyclical failure pattern ('Every January starts the same... too many plans end the same way'), agitates the frustration of repeated restart cycles, then positions Gorilla Bow as the friction-removing solution. The visual narrative mirrors this: ordinary room → product reveal → workout in action → aspirational payoff.
CTA
"Shop now" button paired with a time-limited offer framing: 'For a limited time, get 20% off + 1 month of membership included', urgency and value stacking used to drive immediate click.
Emotional Trigger
Relief + aspiration, viewers feel seen in their pattern of failed restarts, then inspired by the simplicity and accessibility of working out anywhere without a gym membership or dedicated space.
What Makes It Work
The relatable 'living room as gym' environment dismantles the #1 objection (I don't have space or time to get to a gym) by showing the product working in an everyday domestic setting viewers recognize as their own. The PAS copy framework in the primary text surgically targets the annual restart shame loop and positions the product as the structural fix, not willpower, making the purchase feel logical rather than aspirational.
X3 Bar 69 days live Meta Ad Library →
FSA/ HSA READY | TAX FREE GAINS | [Primary text]: Your health, your benefits, your results. Now HSA/FSA eligible, so you can maximize your gains more than ever before without breaking the bank. Just check the reviews. 🤯 | [Headline]: 7,500+ people gave X3 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ | [CTA]: Shop nowMediumStatic-Image
Hook
'FSA/HSA READY' in massive bold white type over orange background, stops the scroll by immediately signaling a financial benefit most fitness shoppers didn't know was available
Angle
Financial optimization meets fitness aspiration, leads with the idea that you can use pre-tax healthcare dollars to buy this equipment, framing it as a 'smart money' purchase rather than an indulgence
Visual Format
Static image combining bold typographic headline, cutout lifestyle/aspirational physique photo of a shirtless athletic male using resistance equipment, and a dark starburst badge callout, layered graphic design with product-in-use context
Copy Framework
Benefit-first with social proof close, leads with the unique FSA/HSA eligibility benefit (rare differentiator), reinforces with 'tax free gains' framing, then closes with 7,500+ five-star reviews as trust validator; mild curiosity gap with 'just check the reviews 🤯'
CTA
'Shop Now', framed urgently by implication of spending pre-tax dollars before they expire, lowering psychological price barrier by reframing cost as 'using benefits you already have'
Emotional Trigger
Relief + aspiration, relief that you can justify the purchase financially using existing benefits, layered with aspiration triggered by the shredded physique demonstrating the product outcome
What Makes It Work
The ad exploits a high-value, underutilized purchase trigger, FSA/HSA eligibility, that instantly reframes price objections by making the buyer feel they're spending money they already set aside for health. Pairing this financial hook with an elite aspirational physique closes both the logical and emotional loops simultaneously.
X3 Bar 34 days live Meta Ad Library →
Primary: 'Here's a timeline of progress photos since I began working out with X3. The first photo is around spring 2021 and the last being some time this morning. It's really easy to fall off routine but I just try and pick up where I left off. Without X3, I would have never achieved my results as I would have never been willing to step in to a gym. X3 brought the gym to me. Forever Grateful.', Christian Shepard | Headline: 'X3 brought the gym to me. Forever Grateful' - Christian Shepard | CTA: Shop NowLowStatic-Image
Hook
A 7-row grid of 35 mirror selfies showing a dramatic physical transformation from soft/untrained to muscular and lean, the sheer volume of progress photos creates an undeniable visual proof pattern that stops the scroll instantly.
Angle
Gym-avoidance barrier removal: leads with the pain of never being willing to step into a gym, positioning X3 as the solution that brought the gym home and made consistent progress finally achievable.
Visual Format
Static image collage, a dense 7x5 grid of real user mirror selfies arranged chronologically top-to-bottom showing month-by-month body transformation progress, set against a bold orange branded border.
Copy Framework
Before/After + Social Proof + Authenticity narrative, uses a real customer testimonial with specific timeline anchoring (Spring 2021 to present), acknowledges relatable struggle (falling off routine), and delivers a single powerful emotional payoff line as the headline.
CTA
'Shop Now', framed passively after an organic, grateful testimonial, allowing the transformation evidence and emotional resolution to do the selling so the CTA feels like a natural next step rather than a push.
Emotional Trigger
Aspiration fused with belonging and relief, the viewer sees themselves in the relatable struggle (gym anxiety, falling off routine) and feels the relief of a solution that removed the barrier, plus aspiration from the visible end-state physique.
What Makes It Work
The sheer volume of 35 sequential progress photos functions as irrefutable, pattern-based social proof that bypasses skepticism, quantity itself becomes the credibility mechanism. The testimonial copy mirrors the internal monologue of the target customer (gym-avoidant, inconsistent) making identification and conversion frictionless.
Treetop Growth Strategy · Evidence AppendixGrowth is a system, not a series of bets.
